_description: Extraction template for Form 1040 Schedule C (Sole Proprietor). Read this before processing any Schedule C. Simplest entity type — all income flows to personal return.

fields.line_30_home_office: SDE-RELEVANT: add back if owner benefit
fields.line_31_net_profit_loss: SDE-CRITICAL: starting point for SDE reconstruction

schedule_c_cogs_section.description: Part III - Cost of Goods Sold (lines 33-42)

fields.line_33_method: Cost / Lower of cost or market / Other

schedule_se.description: Self-Employment Tax — on the 1040, not Schedule C

fields.line_12_se_tax: SDE-RELEVANT: this is the owner's self-employment tax (employer + employee FICA equivalent)

schedule_se.notes: Self-employment tax is NOT an add-back for SDE — it's the owner's personal tax obligation. But it IS relevant for the buyer's realistic cash flow view: a buyer who runs the business as a sole prop will owe this.

rules: There is NO owner salary line item — the owner takes draws, not W-2 wages
Net profit (line 31) IS the owner's compensation. For SDE, you add back discretionary items ON TOP of line 31.
Do NOT add back 'owner salary' because there isn't one — line 31 already captures it
Common add-backs: depreciation (line 13), interest (lines 16a/16b), home office (line 30), personal portion of car (line 9), personal travel/meals
Self-employment tax is a personal obligation, not a business expense — do not add it back

sde_relevant_fields_summary.important: Do NOT add back 'owner salary' — sole props have no salary line. Line 31 IS the owner benefit.
